Panoptikon Desktop

Overview

Control the local Panoptikon service, create databases, and manage Desktop updates.

Local Server

Runs Panoptikon on this computer and keeps it available to the Desktop app.

Server root
Active port
Search UI

Set Up Panoptikon

Choose the folders and indexing options for your first database.

Startup and updates

Run in the background when you sign in
Automatic update checksCheck periodically and notify you when a Desktop update is available

Update status

Loading update status…

Server settings

Settings for the local Panoptikon service. Saving validates the ports and restarts the Server.

Connection

Choose how this computer and, optionally, trusted devices reach Panoptikon.

Used by the Desktop app and browsers on this computer. It is never exposed to the network.
Creates a separate listener for other devices. Desktop-management commands remain available only on this computer.
Trusted networks onlyPanoptikon does not add authentication. Anyone who can reach this port can use the normal API for the databases you select.
A separate port for devices on your network. Your firewall may ask for permission.
Opened when a LAN URL does not specify a database.
Automatically expose new databases, or select an explicit safe subset below.
Databases available on the LAN

Performance and memory

Installation-wide limits shared by every database. Model-specific preload choices remain in each database’s Scan settings.

Inference startup

Keep Python inference workers ready to reduce first-use delay. Prepared workers use additional memory.
After a model is used for the first time, prepare a replacement worker in the background.

Processing limits

More loaders can improve indexing throughput, but increase CPU and memory pressure.
Maximum memory for decoded inputs waiting to be processed, measured in MiB.
MiB
Generated search vectors kept in the Panoptikon Server process. Each entry is usually a few KiB; 1,024 entries typically use only a few MiB and avoid repeated inference for recent queries. Cleared on restart.
entries

Search result cache

Memory for cached search results, kept in the Panoptikon Server process and invalidated automatically when a database changes. Repeated and prefetched search pages are served without re-running the query. 0 disables the cache. Unlike the other settings on this page, “Apply now” takes effect immediately — no restart.
MB
Statistics are available while the Server is running.

Turn this off if you edit Panoptikon’s SQLite files by hand while the Server is running — the cache cannot detect external changes. If you only edit occasionally, a lighter alternative is to leave caching on and clear the cache after editing. Applies on Save and restart.

Desktop integration

Control how Panoptikon opens local files and connects remote Panoptikon pages to this computer.

File opening

Choose the application or command used when Panoptikon opens a file, its folder, or a terminal.

Relay

Allow an approved Panoptikon website—such as one running on a NAS—to open indexed files that are mounted on this computer. Relay listens only on loopback.

Paired endpoints and mappings

Diagnostics

Recent Desktop and Server activity for troubleshooting startup or configuration problems.

Recent logs

The latest 150 log lines. Full log files are available from the Overview page.

No log entries yet.
